title: cyclosorus tonkinensis
intro: Cyclosorus tonkinensis, also known as the Tonkin's maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the fern family (Dryopteridaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Cyclosorus tonkinensis include its potential to alleviate fever, treat respiratory issues, and aid in the treatment of digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
